page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, with a 1:2 CPU-to-memory ratio providing 32.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core). The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX and AVX512F) alongside AES cryptography acceleration. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge consistently in the average performance tier. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1876.98 and a multi-core score of 15440.75. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 831.5 GB/sec, which drops to 84.73 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 54.87 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance handles 15.82 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 16.69 GB/sec. Large language model inference benchmarks show average performance for small 135M models, but prompt processing for medium 7B models drops to a weak tier at 32.03 tokens per second.

Given its 1:2 resource density, this instance offers a compute-focused hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without massive memory footprints. The absence of bundled local storage means storage-heavy applications will require external block storage attachments, which is a key architectural tradeoff. The server is well-suited for compute-bound tasks such as web serving, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is a compute-focused x86_64 server instance powered by a dedicated 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 16 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 CPU-to-memory ratio. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15440.75 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.82 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance during medium-sized LLM prompt processing. This instance is best suited for dedicated compute workloads, web serving, and database operations that do not require high memory density or local storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average compute and memory performance for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.4xlarge is a KVM-based server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated compute-focused instance featuring sixteen Intel Xeon cores and balanced memory bandwidth for web serving and database workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average performance in multi-core processing and Redis operations
Suitable for web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression
